stories.title: Can Your Plants Scream In Pain?
stories.summary: Swedish startup Sonicflora is listening to plants' ultrasonic sounds to detect stress before visible signs appear. By training AI on sounds from dehydrated or diseased plants, they aim to help farmers intervene early. Your next houseplant might just tell you it's thirsty.
stories.extended_summary: Plants scream—just not in a way we can hear. Sonicflora has placed healthy plants in soundproof chambers, introduced stressors like drought, and recorded the ultrasonic 'melodies' they emit. When a plant is dehydrated, tiny air bubbles burst in its tissues, creating airborne signals. The company's AI learns to recognize these distress calls, potentially allowing growers to water or treat crops before any visible damage. So far, they're focusing on indoor tomatoes, but the tech could one day save your cactus from an early grave.
stories.fake_headline: Robot Dog Helps Autistic Children Learn Social Skills in School
